# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_bmw_connected_drive_cleanup_updates_device_instead_of_removing__a1() -> None:
    """Verify cleanup removes config entry id from device entry instead of removing the device.

    The review requested using device_registry.async_update_device(..., remove_config_entry_id=entry.entry_id)
    rather than device_registry.async_remove_device(device.id).
    """
    path = '/workspace/homeassistant/components/bmw_connected_drive/__init__.py'
    source = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'async_remove_device' not in source, 'Device cleanup must not directly remove devices via device_registry.async_remove_device(...). It should instead detach the config entry id from the device so shared devices are not deleted.'

def test_bmw_connected_drive_cleanup_updates_device_instead_of_removing__a2() -> None:
    """Verify cleanup removes config entry id from device entry instead of removing the device.

    The review requested using device_registry.async_update_device(..., remove_config_entry_id=entry.entry_id)
    rather than device_registry.async_remove_device(device.id).
    """
    path = '/workspace/homeassistant/components/bmw_connected_drive/__init__.py'
    source = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
    pattern = re.compile('device_registry\\.async_update_device\\(\\s*device\\.id\\s*,\\s*remove_config_entry_id\\s*=\\s*entry\\.entry_id\\s*\\)', re.DOTALL)
    assert pattern.search(source), 'Expected device cleanup to call device_registry.async_update_device(device.id, remove_config_entry_id=entry.entry_id) for vehicles no longer assigned to the account.'
